title: Etruscan Antefix
description: An antefix depicting the face of the female figure, which is oblong and tapering towards a pronounced chin; the figure's hair is curly, parted in the middle and drawn behind the ears; by the ear-lobes she wears a five-petalled flower on each side.
subject: Antefixes; Architecture; Art; Women; Ceramics; House furnishings

title: Etruscan Mirror
creator: Nerbini Publishing House
description: Illustration of an Etruscan mirror from the book "History of Costumes of Peoples Throughout the Centuries" by Paolo Lorenzini, Nerbini Publishing House, Florence, 1934
subject: Mirrors; Festival Architecture; Festivals; Glassware; House furnishings

title: Etruscan Brooch
description: A bronze fibula with a straight pin and coiled spring. The bow is arched to about midway and then continues as a flattened projection turning up at the end. The arched part of the bow has incised line decoration.
subject: Brooches; Jewelry; House furnishings

title: Etruscan Grave Statute
creator: Feist, Peter H. (Photographer)
description: A photograph of a stone statute recovered from an Etruscan catacomb in a Swiss museum. The statue depicts a young man in robes.
subject: Catacombs; Funeral decorations; Religious Life--Greek Pantheon; Men
source: Media library of Art and Visual History of Humboldt University of Berlin

title: Etruscan Ceramic Vase
description: An Etruscan vase with etchings and two handles
subject: Vases; Ceramics; Art; House furnishings

title: Handbook of Archaeology: Egyptian, Greek, Etruscan, Roman
creator: Westropp, Hodder M.
description: Survey into the architecture of ancient civilizations in modern-day Egypt, Greece, and Italy. Contains depictions and descriptions of architecture pertaining to daily and religious life, including funeary rights
subject: Archaeology; Architecture; Technology; Religious life

title: Etruscan Necropolis of Crocifisso del Tufo
creator: "Tundmatu"
description: Photograph of the exteriror ruins of the Etruscan Necropolis of Crocifisso del Tufo. The facade is mostly in-tact, while the interior has been reduced to rubble.
subject: Catacombs; Religious Life--Greek Pantheon;

title: Etruscan Amphora
description: Reproduction. Turned-out rim; slightly flaring neck; two vertical strap handles from neck to shoulder; elongated ovoid body; flaring foot. Decoration (in dark brown to red paint): the upper side of the turned-out rim is painted with a zig-zag pattern, the underside with six thin horizontal lines; on neck a reclining s-pattern below which an area with two rows of thin triangles bordered by three horizontal lines both above and below; on handles four vertical lines with four horizontal ones at each attachment.
subject: Amphoras; Ceramics; House furnishings; Reproductions
source: The Museum of Mediterranean and Near Eastern Antiquities

title: Etruscan Miniature Ornament
description: Reproduction of agold minatiure figurine feature in a feline body and human-like head.
subject: Ornaments; House furnishings

title: Etruscan Votive Sculpture
description: A cermaic votive fragment of a foot. Likely part of a larger figure or model.
subject: Votive offerings; Festival architecture; Festivals; Religious life; Ceramics

title: Etruscan Stamnos
description: Fabricated around 500 BC by the Micali Painter, a prominent Etruscan black-figure vase painter with a orkshop in Vulci, named after the pioneer of Etruscan archaeology, Giuseppe Micali. The neck is covered in black slip, with a running horse and a winged griffin between the handles.
subject: Art; Ceramics; Religious life

title: Tour to the Sepulchres of Etruria
creator: Gray, Elizabeth C.
description: Detailed recount of a tour of the catacombs of Etruria.
subject: Catacombs; Funeral decorations; Religious Life--Greek Pantheon

title: Etruscan Tomb Fragment
description: Fragment of the tomb of Nikarete from Athens. The fragment depicts a veiled woman looking slightly downwad longoingly.
subject: Catacombs; Architecture; Funeral decorations; Religious Life--Greek Pantheon; Masonry

title: Funerary Stele
description: Tombstone with name inscription: Silenis (daughter of Myiskos from Boeotia) with a small servant girl.
subject: Catacombs; Architecture; Funeral decorations; Religious Life--Greek Pantheon; Masonry

title: Etruscan Helmet
creator: Bromet, William
description: The drawing shows a bronze helmet with a crest and ear pieces, decorated with circles, found in making a road between Innsbruck and Bixen, and thought by Dr Bromet to be of Etruscan form.
subject: Armor; Helmets; Reproductions
